prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis completed on nitrobenzene using DFT B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard', PySCF) in the gas phase, neutral singlet (charge 0, multiplicity 1). Input geometry from `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz` was used as a single point (not re-optimized). The calculation computed Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptors for all 14 atoms by evaluating Mulliken partial charges on three states: neutral parent, cation (charge +1, doublet), and anion (charge −1, doublet). SCF converged for all three states. Charge conservation checks passed: sum(f⁺) = 1.0000 and sum(f⁻) = 1.0000, confirming reliable N±1 state geometries. The most electrophilic atom is carbon (C-4, ipso position) with f⁺ = 0.4964; the most nucleophilic atom is carbon (C-5, ortho position) with f⁻ = 0.1073. The headline Fukui electrophilicity index for the most reactive position (ipso carbon) is 0.49642 (rounded to 5 sig figs). Integrity status: ok, trustworthy. A PNG bar chart of per-atom f⁺/f⁻/f⁰ was generated. No warnings were reported.</prose>
